mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increase in RSAFS over three months signals strong consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward rank IC of RSAFS growth against 20-day returns remains below 0.03, or the top quartile of growth does not yield significantly higher positive return frequency than the bottom quartile (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 2.0% and continues to increase over three months, monetary tightening compresses equity valuation multiples and depresses S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When the trigger is active, 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A negative spread between the federal funds rate and the unemployment rate (FEDFUNDS - UNRATE < -2%) indicates accommodative policy relative to labor slack, which supports equity risk appetite and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The rank IC of the FEDFUNDS-UNRATE spread against 20-day returns stays below 0.03, or the spread shows no significant correlation in a multivariate regression (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion, rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals capacity constraints and overheating, prompting market expectations of tightening and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In ex post identified late-cycle periods, rising INDPRO does not precede negative returns in a majority of cases, or the rolling 2-year rank IC of INDPRO growth is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS monthly increase exceeding 200,000) signals a robust labor market that boosts consumer sentiment and spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns within the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns following strong payrolls (>200k) are not significantly higher than those following weak payrolls (Mann-Whitney U test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) drop below 1.2 million units (annualized), it signals a housing market downturn that depresses construction, consumer wealth, and overall economic activity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months when HOUST falls below 1200, forward 20-day returns are positive more than 40% of the time or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When annual CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) exceeds the federal funds rate by more than one percentage point, the resulting negative real interest rate reduces the discount rate on equity cash flows and boosts S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The real interest rate (CPI minus FEDFUNDS) has a rank IC below 0.03 against 20-day returns, or its coefficient in a regression is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) reinforces expectations of further rate hikes, which compresses equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods identified as tightening cycles, CPI acceleration does not lead to negative 60-day forward returns in at least 60% of instances, or the rolling 2-year rank IC is non-negative.
